paper_metadata.title: Zero-Shot Text-to-Image Generation
paper_metadata.abstract: Text-to-image generation has traditionally focused on finding better modeling assumptions for training on a fixed dataset. These assumptions might involve complex architectures, auxiliary losses, or side information such as object part labels or segmentation masks supplied during training. We describe a simple approach for this task based on a transformer that autoregressively models the text and image tokens as a single stream of data. With sufficient data and scale, our approach is competitive with previous domain-specific models when evaluated in a zero-shot fashion.

extraction_data.innovation: Zero-shot text-to-image generation using a Transformer-based model trained on a diverse text corpus, enabling generation of images from text prompts without task-specific fine-tuning.
extraction_data.pretraining_task: Discrete Variational Autoencoder (VAE) for image tokenization
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Large-scale text and image dataset (not specified in paper)
extraction_data.extension: Uses parameter sharding and PowerSGD for mixed-precision training to reduce memory usage and improve scalability.
extraction_data.research_problem: Zero-shot text-to-image generation without task-specific training

extraction_data.innovation: Zero-shot text-to-image generation using a transformer-based model with gradient scaling to avoid underflow and stabilize training.
extraction_data.pretraining_corpus: Conceptual Captions, filtered subset of YFCC100M, and internet text-image pairs
extraction_data.hardware_used: NVIDIA V100 GPUs (as inferred from the context of training on large models)
extraction_data.extension: Gradient scaling to prevent underflow during training by scaling gradients in each transformer block.
extraction_data.application: Text-to-image generation, image generation from text prompts
